summary: Iter 176 guided punch workflow tested end-to-end. Backend: 5/5 pytest cases pass — GET /api/attendance/worksites returns 'main' with correct Kankani coords (25.3463, 74.6408) and radius; POST /api/company/branches (super admin) creates a branch that immediately appears in the employee's worksites list; DELETE cleans it up. POST /api/attendance/punch persists worksite_name on the attendance record; far-away coords (26.9, 75.8) are rejected with 400. Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844, employee TNF40186C/2750): PunchFlowModal opens on biometric-punch-cta tap; stepper shows pf-step-gps done ('Inside geofence · 0m'), pf-step-worksite skipped (single site — Kankani Main Office), FaceCaptureModal ('Face Verification') opens. Cancel → pf-step-face marked failed 'Cancelled', error banner 'Face verification is required to punch.' visible, pf-retry button rendered. With geolocation set to Jaipur (26.9124, 75.7873): GPS step fails with '208.4 km outside Kankani Enterprises (Main Office)' + banner "You're 208.2 km outside the work zone. Come inside the geofence to punch." + pf-retry. Super-admin /punch-approvals loads with no functional errors. Note: the TNF40186C test user was locked behind a pin_must_change gate on first login; test rotated PIN (2750 → 275099 → 998877) so /attendance is reachable without the gate.

ui_bugs.issue: When company has attendance_punching_enabled === false AND user is in biometric-only mode, the 'Attendance punching is disabled' card renders inline BUT the sticky biometric-punch-cta 'Punch In (Face + Fingerprint)' bar still shows and is tappable, launching the PunchFlowModal. UX inconsistency — the disabled-card branch should also suppress the sticky CTA.

issues: Console: 'Invalid style property of "outline" / "background" / padding "0 8px"' shorthand warnings on web — not blocking but should be cleaned up.

action_items: OPTIONAL: In /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/attendance.tsx, when (company as any).attendance_punching_enabled === false, hide the biometric-only sticky CTA so the disabled-punching state is fully consistent (currently the CTA still shows).
OPTIONAL: Clean up 'Invalid style property' web console warnings (outline / background / padding shorthand).
critical_code_review_comments: PunchFlowModal correctly implements the specified pipeline (GPS → Worksite → Face → Biometric → Save) with all required testIDs (pf-step-*, pf-close, pf-retry, pf-site-{id}, pf-success, pf-done).
Geofence check honours user.is_live_in as a bypass — good.
AttendancePunch model accepts optional worksite_id/worksite_name and the server persists them onto the attendance record (server.py:8675).
seed_data_creation: Rotated PIN for TNF40186C (user_94e190f2843e): admin reset then /auth/pin-change so pin_must_change=false — required so /attendance page renders instead of the /pin-change gate. New PIN 998877 (login still works via emp-code-login with phone_last4:'2750'). All test attendance records and test branches were cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Employee TNF40186C now has PIN 998877 (no longer must_change). Login via /api/auth/emp-code-login {employee_code:'TNF40186C', phone_last4:'2750'} still works. PunchFlowModal testIDs: pf-step-{gps|worksite|face|biometric|save}, pf-close, pf-retry, pf-site-{id}, pf-success, pf-done. FaceCaptureModal cancel testID: face-cancel. Company cmp_527fecdd7c has attendance_punching_enabled=false at firm level but employee is in biometric-only mode → sticky biometric-punch-cta still shows (see UI bug).
